response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong software craftsman with genuine geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map GIS) and distributed systems exposure (high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby), but the role's core requirement is modern, production-grade C++ or Rust, and his primary stack is firmly TypeScript/Node.js. His C++ usage is both brief (6 months at National Instruments, 2014–2015) and dated, with no Rust listed at all. The geospatial work he has done is focused on data collection and display rather than routing graph construction, tile optimization, or latency-sensitive navigation pipelines, leaving a significant domain gap alongside the language gap.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map) demonstrating familiarity with geo-data concepts
Distributed systems and high-throughput service background (millions of req/s, async I/O, low-latency design at Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development consistently applied across roles
concerns: Primary commercial stack is TypeScript/Node.js; C++ appears only in a 6-month role from 2014 and a student project — far below the commercial C++ depth required
No Rust experience anywhere in the resume
No experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, or related navigation guidance domains
